text:
Wea
already
know
that integers can
n be
represented
on a number
line.
To do this, we
first choose a
point and
mark it as
 0, called t
the origin.
Moving
 one unit to the right
gives the point representing
g 1, two units to
the right
gives 2, and so on. Similarly,
moving
unit to the left of the
onei
origin
gives -1, two units to the left
gives -2, and so on. Each integer lies
at an equal
distance
from the next
t one. This is r
 represented in Fig. 3.3.
